medical_history.history_of_present_illness: Diagnosed with glutaric aciduria type I through newborn screening
medical_history.past_medical_history: Product of consanguineous marriage, head circumference at birth was 37.3 cm (>90th centile)
medical_history.social_history: Compliance with the treatment protocol and clinic follow-up has been poor

laboratory_examinations.findings: 6 newborns were diagnosed as having GA I, giving an incidence of 1:65,275 (CI: 1:29,988 = 1:177,861)

laboratory_examinations.procedure_name: Acylcarnitine profiles and urinary GA and 3HGA levels

radiographic_examinations.findings: Increased extra-axial spaces around the cerebral convexities and widening of the sulci, most prominently the sylvian fissures, but otherwise normal brain structure

Bilateral small anterior temporal arachnoid cysts with symmetrically enlarged sylvian fissures. The third and lateral ventricles were moderately dilated. There was generalised abnormal low density of the white matter throughout the supratentorial cerebral hemispheres and low density within the putamina bilaterally

Extensive periventricular and frontal lobe white matter signal abnormality, putamenal signal abnormality and atrophy and associated bitemporal arachnoid cyst. Overall there had been no significant progression of pathology on neuroimaging compared with the earlier brain CT. There was no significant cortical atrophy or increase in the extra-axial spaces
radiographic_examinations.changes_over_time: No significant progression of pathology compared to earlier brain CT

other_tests.findings: Patients 2, 3 and 5 had full-scale IQ levels in the average to very high average range (FSIQ patient 2: 109, patient 3: 124, patient 5: 106). None of these patients showed any deficits on tests measuring attention, memory or executive function. On the MABC-2, all three patients had average results on the Aiming & Catching and Balance subtests; however, all had significant impairments on the Manual Dexterity portion (SS—patient 2:5, patient 3:4, patient 5:6). Patient 4 was significantly impaired on all measures (FSIQ = 62, SS narrative memory = 1, SS Manual Dexterity = 1). All patients were found to have speech difficulties
